title: Denmark's Election Leaves Prime Minister's Future Uncertain
summary: In Denmark, an election happened on Tuesday, but the results were not clear. This means no group won enough votes to decide who will be the prime minister. The current prime minister, Mette Frederiksen, leads the Social Democrats party, but they got fewer votes than before. Another important person, Lars Løkke Rasmussen, leads the Moderate party and can help decide who becomes prime minister. He wants everyone to work together. The election focused on issues like money and living costs, not on a big problem about Greenland and the U.S. president. Denmark is a small country in Europe with about 6 million people, and it is part of groups like the European Union and NATO. The prime minister is known for helping Ukraine and having strict rules on migration. Now, people are waiting to see if she can stay as prime minister or if someone else will take over.

background_read: Denmark is a country in Europe with about 6 million people. It is part of the European Union, which helps countries trade and work together, and NATO, which is a defense group. Elections happen when people vote to choose their leaders, like the prime minister. Sometimes, no party gets enough votes to win alone, so they must work with others to form a government. This article talks about an election where the results were unclear, so the future leader is uncertain.
Article_Structure: WHO: Mette Frederiksen (prime minister), Lars Løkke Rasmussen (foreign minister), Social Democrats party, Moderate party
WHAT: An election in Denmark ended with unclear results, leaving the prime minister's future uncertain
WHERE: Denmark, a country in Europe
WHY: Because no political group won a majority, and issues like money and living costs were important in the campaign

perspectives.description: Lars Løkke Rasmussen wants all parties to cooperate to form a government.
